content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 18, 2007

John J. Ruprecht
Attorney at Law
P O Box 1445
Fort Bragg, California 95437

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-066

This letter is in response to your request for written advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090. We also offer no opinion on the application of Health and Safety Code Section 32121.3 to Government Code Section 1090.

The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be untimely or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).) Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(b)(7).) The facts stated in your letter imply two questions.

We decline to provide written advice regarding the propriety of Dr. Kermen having entered into a contract with the Mendocino Coast Health Care District. This question involves activities that have already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective, efficient administration of the Act mandated by Section 83111. (See Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request.

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-07-066
Page No. 2

With respect to the question of whether Dr. Kermen has a conflict of interest as a
board member of the district while under contract with the district, the Act's general
conflict-of-interest rule is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official
makes a governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect
on one or more of his or her financial interests. (Government Code Section 87100.)You
have not provided facts regarding any governmental decisions coming before the board
that may affect Dr. Kermen's financial interests. Accordingly, we cannot provide further
guidance at this time. If a particular governmental decision should come up, you may
wish to write to us at that time.

For a general discussion of the application of the Act's conflict-of-interest
provisions we are enclosing a copy of the Commission's fact sheet entitled "Can I Vote?
Conflicts of Interest Overview."

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division

VJ:jgl
